Let me compile the findings: ## Performance Hotspot Analysis Report Based on my thorough exploration of `/home/diogenes/Projects/keating/web/src`, here are the key rendering performance hotspots: --- ### 1. MARKDOWN/MATH/SYNTAX-HIGHLIGHTING LIBRARIES LOADED AT TOP-LEVEL **Critical**: These heavy libraries are imported synchronously at module top-level in **two places**, creating redundant bundle weight: **File 1: `/home/diogenes/Projects/keating/web/src/components/MarkdownBlock.tsx` (Lines 2-19)** - Imports `react-markdown` (line 2) - Imports `react-syntax-highlighter` with 10 language packs eagerly loaded (lines 7-19): - tsx, javascript, typescript, python, bash, json, css, markup, yaml, rust, go - Uses `PrismLight` which is lighter than full Prism, but still registers all languages **at module load time** (lines 23-42) - Imports `rehype-katex` (line 6) for KaTeX rendering - Imports `remark-math`, `remark-gfm` (lines 4-5) **File 2: `/home/diogenes/Projects/keating/web/src/components/AssistantChatPanel.tsx` (Lines 32-35)** - Duplicates `ReactMarkdown`, `remark-gfm`, `remark-math`, `rehype-katex` imports - **Additionally**: Uses `ReactMarkdown` inline in 19 `.map()` callbacks (line 1164-1279) with **new `components` objects defined in every segment render** (lines 1168-1276), causing unnecessary re-parsing **Top-level CSS import**: - `/home/diogenes/Projects/keating/web/src/main-react.tsx` (line 4): `import "katex/dist/katex.min.css"` — loads entire KaTeX stylesheet eagerly even if no math is on the page **Impact**: Syntax highlighter + KaTeX + markdown libs add ~500KB+ to initial bundle and block parsing until loaded. --- ### 2. LARGEST/HEAVIEST COMPONENTS **QuizRenderer.tsx** (1,270 lines) - **Lines 1241-1257**: Renders all visible quiz questions in a `.map()` without virtualization: ```tsx {visibleQuestions.map((q, i) => ( ))} ``` - **Problem**: When quiz has 50+ questions, all `QuestionCard` DOM nodes exist in memory simultaneously after reveal - **Missing**: No virtualization (e.g., `react-window`, `react-virtualized`) - **No memoization** on `QuestionCard` component itself - **Lines 886-888**: `visibleQuestions` is memoized but only depends on `quiz.questions` and `skippedIds` - **Lines 1014-1020, 1022-1028, 1030-1038**: Heavy computations in `partialCredits`, `rawScore`, `weightedScore` run on every render even when not needed - `partialCredits` loops all scorable questions (line 1015-1020) - `rawScore` manually iterates again (line 1023-1028) - `weightedScore` iterates a third time (line 1032-1037) **Chat.tsx** (574 lines) - Light wrapper around `useKeatingAgent()` and `AssistantChatPanel` - 11 state variables (lines 64-80) — well-organized - Modal/menu overlays re-render on every keystroke (mobile menu, artifact browser, settings) **AssistantChatPanel.tsx** (3,057 lines) — EXTREMELY HEAVY - **Largest file in the app** - **3,010-3,012**: Three `useState` hooks for `Agent`, `callbacks`, `version` — one of the deepest state trees - **2,596-2,602**: `threadComponents` memoization depends on `UserMessageComponent` and `AssistantMessageComponent` (which are not memoized, see below) - **Lines 1154-1161**: Parses segments and renders via `parseInteractiveSegments()` + `renderInteractiveSegment()`: ```tsx const segments = parseInteractiveSegments(displayText).filter(...) {segments.map((seg, i) => { const card = renderInteractiveSegment(seg, i); if (card !== null) return card; return })} ``` - **Problem**: New `components` object created on **every render** for every segment (lines 1168-1276) - **Problem**: If a message has 10 segments, creates 10 new `ReactMarkdown` trees even if text hasn't changed - **Lines 1160-1281**: Inline 116-line custom `components` prop redefined each time `MarkdownText` renders **ProvidersModelsTab.tsx** (883 lines) - Heavy form UI with 4 independent state objects (lines 83-105) - Each form change re-parses provider discovery (line 210-216) asynchronously - No debouncing on provider/model form inputs **SceneRenderer.tsx** - **Lines 24-78**: `parseStoryboard()` called without memoization — re-parses markdown on every scene change - Uses regex matching on entire markdown blob (line 743) --- ### 3. CHAT STREAMING & TOKEN RE-RENDERS **useKeatingAgent.tsx** (hooks into agent runtime) - Uses `@earendil-works/pi-agent-core` Agent which streams tokens via `hybridStreamFn` (line 36) - Agent state includes full `messages` array (line 279) — every token causes agent state update - **Problem**: `AssistantChatPanel` receives updated agent on every token, re-renders entire `MessagePrimitive.Root` tree - **Lines 2615, 2764, 2913**: `MessagePrimitive.Content` gets new `components` object on every message update (not memoized) **StreamingTextPart component** (Lines 294-364 in AssistantChatPanel) - **Lines 307-343**: Uses `useState` + `setInterval` to animate visible text character-by-character - **Problem**: Every 18ms (line 335) calls `setVisibleText()`, causing parent re-render of the entire message - **Line 312-316**: Checks `displayText.startsWith(previousTextRef.current)` — if user edits above, triggers full re-animation **MarkdownText** (Lines 1146-1285) - **Not memoized** — re-renders and re-parses on every parent update - Calls `stripArtifactLinks()` (line 1153) on every render - Calls `parseInteractiveSegments()` (line 1154) on every render without caching --- ### 4. MEMOIZATION GAPS & RE-RENDER TRIGGERS **Missing Memoization in High-Frequency Components**: - `UserMessageComponent` (line ~2750) — renders user avatars, not memoized - `AssistantMessageComponent` (line ~2903) — renders with dynamic feedback state (lines 2849-2851), not memoized - `MarkdownText` — used in 2 places, never memoized (line 357, line 1164) - `QuestionCard` (line 432 in QuizRenderer) — used in `.map()`, not memoized - `CodeBlock` in MarkdownBlock (line 115) — renders syntax highlighter, not memoized **State Triggers Whole-Tree Re-renders**: - `feedback` state (line 2849) — user clicks thumbs up/down → entire message re-renders - `copied` state (line 1071) — copy button → message re-renders - `open` state (line 2130) — reasoning collapse → re-parses reasoning - `localVersion` (line 2253) — bumped on any attachment/composer change → may cause thread re-render --- ### 5. INLINE FUNCTIONS & OBJECT CREATION **19 inline `.map()` with arrow functions in AssistantChatPanel**: - Line 1160-1281: Each segment render creates new inline callback - Lines 2629-2635: Map over quiz questions creating new objects in every render - Creates new `customEvent` detail objects on every message feedback **Dynamic `components` prop for ReactMarkdown**: - **AssistantChatPanel line 1168-1276**: 116-line object recreated per segment - **MarkdownBlock line 220-280**: 60-line object recreated on every render (but at least it's in a `const COMPONENTS` at line 220-280... actually **line 220 shows it IS constant** — good) - Actually: Line 282 wraps it in `useMemo()`, so this is OK --- ### 6. QUIZ RENDERING PERFORMANCE ISSUES **Line 1241-1257 (All Questions at Once)**: ```tsx {visibleQuestions.map((q, i) => ( ))} ``` - After `revealed=true`, renders **all visible questions** as `QuestionCard` components - Each `QuestionCard` has: - Conditional rendering for 8 question types (lines 548-722) - Confidence slider re-created per question (lines 725-730) - No virtualization for paginated views - **Impact**: 50-question quiz = 50 full DOM nodes + 50 state objects in parent **Expensive Scoring Computation** (Lines 1014-1038): - `partialCredits` (1014-1020): Iterates all questions, calls `questionCredit()` for each (complex function, lines 122-170) - `rawScore` (1022-1028): Iterates all questions, calls `isCorrect()` for each - `weightedScore` (1030-1038): Iterates all questions, multiplies by confidence - **All three run on every QuizRenderer render**, even if answers haven't changed - **Should**: Memoize with `useMemo(..., [answers, confidence, scorableQuestions])` --- ### 7. UNOPTIMIZED IMAGES & ASSETS **Images loaded without lazy-loading**: - `/brand/logo-lockup.png` (Chat.tsx line 214, Chat.tsx line 216) - `/brand/mascot-head.png` (AssistantChatPanel line 2906) — renders on every assistant message - Profile images loaded as data URLs (AssistantChatPanel line 2754) **No `loading="lazy"` attributes** on any `` tags --- ### 8. ANIMATION & SCENE RENDERING **SceneRenderer.tsx**: - **Line 24-78**: `parseStoryboard()` regex parsing happens on every scene change without caching - Uses Mermaid (optional, but imported dynamically — good) - 3D scenes via Three.js/React Three Fiber (not directly analyzed, but heavy framework) **AnimatedScene** (referenced line 1014): - Animation parsing happens in `renderInteractiveSegment()` (line 1009-1014) - Could be memoized per animation ID **Mermaid rendering**: - MermaidRenderer.tsx uses dynamic import (good for code-splitting) - But re-renders mermaid diagram on every diagram update without debounce --- ### SUMMARY TABLE | Component | Lines | Issue | Severity | |-----------|-------|-------|----------| | MarkdownBlock.tsx | 2-19 | Syntax highlighter + 10 langs loaded eagerly at module init | HIGH | | AssistantChatPanel.tsx | 1168-1276 | 116-line `components` object recreated per segment render | HIGH | | AssistantChatPanel.tsx | 1154-1161 | `parseInteractiveSegments()` called without caching on every render | HIGH | | QuizRenderer.tsx | 1241-1257 | All quiz questions rendered without virtualization | HIGH | | QuizRenderer.tsx | 1014-1038 | Scoring computations re-run 3x per render without memoization | MEDIUM | | AssistantChatPanel.tsx | 2596-2602 | `threadComponents` depends on non-memoized UserMessage/AssistantMessage | MEDIUM | | MarkdownText | 1146-1285 | Not memoized, re-parses on every parent update | MEDIUM | | main-react.tsx | 4 | KaTeX CSS imported globally even if no math on page | MEDIUM | | StreamingTextPart | 307-343 | Character animation updates parent 55x/sec | MEDIUM | | Syntax highlighter | Line 7 | `PrismLight` registers 10 languages at module load | MEDIUM | | Chat images | Various | No lazy-loading, mascot renders per message | LOW | --- ### KEY OPPORTUNITIES 1. **Lazy-load syntax highlighter** — only load when first code block needed 2. **Memoize MarkdownText + custom `components` objects** per segment 3. **Cache `parseInteractiveSegments()`** results with text hash 4. **Virtualize QuizRenderer** revealed state (use `react-window` for 100+ questions) 5. **Split memoized scoring** in QuizRenderer: `useMemo(partialCredits)`, separate from state updates 6. **Defer KaTeX CSS** — load only when math detected 7. **Memoize message components** — wrap `UserMessageComponent` and `AssistantMessageComponent` with `React.memo()` 8. **Debounce StreamingTextPart** animation or use CSS animation instead of JS 9. **Add `loading="lazy"` to images** and consider responsive image formats 10. **Cache markdown segment parsing** with WeakMap keyed by text content

# Web Rendering Performance & Load-Time Plan

## Context

The web app (`web/`, Vite 8 + React + TanStack Router) has grown to a **57MB `dist/`** with a **4.3MB main chunk**. The PWA precache limit was bumped 10MB → 20MB twice in recent commits (`5138034`, `4a00fef`) — a sign the team is working around bundle bloat rather than fixing it. The root cause: `web/src/App.tsx:10-19` imports all 10 route pages eagerly, so visiting the Landing page pulls in `Chat` + the 3,057-line `AssistantChatPanel` + markdown/KaTeX/syntax-highlighter. Runtime rendering also re-parses markdown and recreates component objects on every token during streaming.

Goal: faster first paint on Landing, leaner per-route chunks, smarter caching, and fewer wasted re-renders during chat/quiz — without breaking the PWA offline story. User asked to prioritize **all four**: measure first, then load time, caching, and runtime rendering.

The work is phased so each phase is independently shippable and measurable.

---

## Phase 0 — Measure first (baseline)

- Add `rollup-plugin-visualizer` as a dev dependency and wire it into `web/vite.config.ts` `plugins` (gated behind an `ANALYZE` env var so normal builds are unaffected), emitting `dist/stats.html` with `gzipSize: true, brotliSize: true`.
- Add a `"build:analyze": "ANALYZE=1 vite build"` script to `web/package.json`.
- Run it to capture current chunk sizes (main 4.3MB, `typescript` 3.3MB, `reward` 2.6MB, etc.) as the baseline to compare against after each phase.

Install with **bun** per repo convention: `bun add -d rollup-plugin-visualizer`.

---

## Phase 1 — Route-level code splitting (biggest load-time win)

**File:** `web/src/App.tsx`

Convert the 10 eager page imports (lines 10-19) to lazy route components. TanStack Router code-based routing supports this via `lazyRouteComponent`:

```ts
import { lazyRouteComponent } from "@tanstack/react-router";
const Landing = lazyRouteComponent(() => import("./pages/Landing"));
// ...one per page
```

Keep `Landing` eager **or** lazy with a lightweight inline fallback (it's the entry page; test both — lazy adds one round-trip but shrinks the initial JS). Pass each to `createRoute({ component })`. Add a `defaultPendingComponent` / Suspense fallback on the router or root route so route transitions show a spinner instead of blank.

Expected effect: Landing no longer ships `Chat`/`AssistantChatPanel`/markdown stack; each route becomes its own chunk fetched on navigation.

---

## Phase 2 — Lazy-load the heavy markdown/highlight/math stack

**Files:** `web/src/components/MarkdownBlock.tsx` (lines 2-42), `web/src/components/AssistantChatPanel.tsx` (lines 32-35), `web/src/main-react.tsx:4`

- `MarkdownBlock.tsx` eagerly imports `react-syntax-highlighter` (PrismLight) + 10 language packs + `vscDarkPlus` style and registers them at module load. Wrap the `SyntaxHighlighter` usage in a `React.lazy` boundary (a `LazyCodeBlock` that `import()`s the highlighter + languages on first code block), so pages with no code blocks never load it.
- KaTeX CSS is imported globally in `main-react.tsx:4` (`katex/dist/katex.min.css`). Move it to load alongside the markdown renderer (import inside the chat/markdown chunk) rather than the global entry, so Landing doesn't pay for it.
- Deduplicate the markdown plugin imports — both `MarkdownBlock` and `AssistantChatPanel` independently import `react-markdown` + `remark-*` + `rehype-katex`. Confirm they share one chunk after Phase 1 (they will if both land in route chunks that import a shared `MarkdownText` module). Consider extracting a single shared `MarkdownText` component to guarantee one copy.

---

## Phase 3 — Manual vendor chunking

**File:** `web/vite.config.ts` (`build.rollupOptions.output.manualChunks`)

`manualChunks` was previously removed for rolldown compatibility, but the project is on **standard Vite 8 / Rollup** (`vite ^8.0.8`, no `rolldown-vite` override), so it's safe to reintroduce. Add a function-form `manualChunks` to isolate large, independently-cached vendors:

- `three` / `@react-three/*` → already lazy via `KeatingHero3D`; ensure it stays its own chunk.
- `katex` + `react-syntax-highlighter` → `markdown-vendor`.
- `recharts` / `mermaid` → their own chunks (mermaid already dynamic).
- `@google/genai`, agent SDKs → `ai-vendor`.
- Leave `@huggingface/transformers`, `pdfjs-dist`, `onnxruntime-web`, TypeScript compiler as-is (already dynamically imported).

Goal: stable long-cached vendor chunks so app-code changes don't bust vendor caches. Verify against the Phase 0 visualizer output that no chunk regresses.

---

## Phase 4 — Caching & PWA tuning

**Files:** `web/vite.config.ts` (VitePWA workbox, lines 291-355), `web/nitro.config.ts`

- Nitro already sets 1-year cache on hashed `/dist/` assets (`nitro.config.ts:32`); confirm `immutable` is present in the `Cache-Control` value, add if missing.
- Add `woff2`/`woff` runtime caching (CacheFirst, long expiry) to workbox `runtimeCaching` so fonts survive SW updates.
- Re-evaluate `maximumFileSizeToCacheInBytes: 20MB`: after Phases 1-3 the per-file max should drop. Lower it back toward 8-10MB so giant one-off chunks (e.g. the TS compiler) are runtime-cached on demand instead of force-precached on first load — this cuts the install/precache cost dramatically.
- Keep `cleanupOutdatedCaches`, `skipWaiting`, `clientsClaim` as-is.

---

## Phase 5 — Runtime rendering (chat & quiz re-renders)

**File:** `web/src/components/AssistantChatPanel.tsx`
- Wrap `UserMessageComponent` and `AssistantMessageComponent` in `React.memo`.
- Memoize/extract the 116-line `components` object recreated per segment (lines ~1168-1276) to a stable module-level/`useMemo` const.
- Cache `parseInteractiveSegments(displayText)` + `stripArtifactLinks` results (e.g. `useMemo` keyed on `displayText`, or a module `WeakMap`/`Map` by text) so streaming tokens don't re-parse the whole message tree each tick.
- `StreamingTextPart` (lines ~307-343) updates parent ~55×/sec via `setInterval`. Throttle the interval or move the reveal to CSS animation to avoid re-rendering the full message subtree per frame.

**File:** `web/src/components/QuizRenderer.tsx`
- Wrap the three scoring computations (`partialCredits`, `rawScore`, `weightedScore`, lines ~1014-1038) in `useMemo` keyed on `[answers, confidence, scorableQuestions]`.
- Wrap `QuestionCard` in `React.memo`.
- Virtualization (react-window) for 50+ question quizzes is optional — defer unless quizzes are routinely that large.

---

## Verification

After each phase:
1. `cd web && bun run build:analyze` → open `dist/stats.html`, compare chunk sizes to the Phase 0 baseline. Target: Landing initial JS well under the current 4.3MB; markdown/katex/highlighter absent from the entry chunk.
2. `cd web && bun run typecheck` → no type regressions (lazy route conversion is the main risk).
3. `cd web && bun test` → existing tests pass (`web/test/`, `web/src/test/`).
4. `bun run dev`, then in the browser DevTools Network panel: load `/` cold (disable cache) and confirm the initial JS payload dropped and `Chat`/markdown chunks load only when navigating to `/chat`.
5. Lighthouse (or DevTools Performance) before/after on `/` and `/chat` for FCP/LCP/TBT deltas.
6. PWA: build + `bun run preview`, confirm the service worker installs without exceeding the precache budget and the app works offline after first load.

## Risks / notes
- Lazy routes need a Suspense/pending fallback or transitions flash blank — must be wired in Phase 1.
- Reintroducing `manualChunks` was previously reverted; validate the build completes and chunks don't double-load shared deps via the visualizer.
- Lowering the precache limit changes offline-first behavior for huge chunks (they become on-demand) — acceptable since those (TS compiler, transformers) are already feature-gated, but call it out when shipping.

## Phase 3 — Migrate to rolldown-vite + vendor chunking

The earlier `manualChunks` was removed "for rolldown compatibility," so the team is already pointed at rolldown. **rolldown-vite** is a drop-in, Rust-based replacement for Vite's Rollup/esbuild pipeline — meaningfully faster builds (relevant given the noted Docker build stalls and timeouts) and a more capable webpack-style chunk splitter.

**Migration (`web/package.json`):** alias Vite to rolldown-vite. Vite is a direct dependency here, so:

```jsonc
// Pin an exact version — rolldown-vite is experimental and can break in patches.
"devDependencies": { "vite": "npm:rolldown-vite@<pinned-version>" }
```

Reinstall with bun (`bun install`). No config changes required to *start*; then address caveats:

- **Plugin compatibility is the main risk** — this project has several custom/native-adjacent plugins: `nodepod`, `chatProxyPlugin`, `manimWebPublicPlugin`, `VitePWA`, `@vitejs/plugin-react`, `@tailwindcss/vite`. Build once and watch for option-validation warnings and any plugin that misbehaves; report rolldown-specific issues to `vitejs/rolldown-vite`, not core Vite.
- If anything in `transformWithEsbuild`/esbuild options is used, note esbuild is now an optional peer dep (prefer `transformWithOxc`).

**Vendor chunking via `advancedChunks`** (rolldown's preferred API, replaces deprecated `manualChunks`) in `web/vite.config.ts` `build.rollupOptions.output`:

```js
advancedChunks: {
  groups: [
    { name: 'markdown-vendor', test: /\/node_modules\/(katex|react-syntax-highlighter)\// },
    { name: 'three-vendor',    test: /\/node_modules\/(three|@react-three)\// },
    { name: 'charts-vendor',   test: /\/node_modules\/(recharts|d3-)/ },
    { name: 'ai-vendor',       test: /\/node_modules\/(@google\/genai|@earendil-works)\// },
  ]
}
```

Leave `@huggingface/transformers`, `pdfjs-dist`, `onnxruntime-web`, TypeScript compiler, and `mermaid` as-is — already dynamically imported, so they're naturally split. Goal: stable long-cached vendor chunks so app-code changes don't bust vendor caches.

**Fallback:** if rolldown-vite breaks a plugin we can't quickly resolve, stay on standard Vite 8 and use the deprecated-but-supported function-form `manualChunks` with the same groupings — chunking still lands, just without the rolldown build-speed win. Validate either path against the Phase 0 visualizer (no chunk should regress or double-load shared deps).

# Web Rendering Performance & Load-Time Plan

## Context

The web app (`web/`, Vite 8 + React + TanStack Router) has grown to a **57MB `dist/`** with a **4.3MB main chunk**. The PWA precache limit was bumped 10MB → 20MB twice in recent commits (`5138034`, `4a00fef`) — a sign the team is working around bundle bloat rather than fixing it. The root cause: `web/src/App.tsx:10-19` imports all 10 route pages eagerly, so visiting the Landing page pulls in `Chat` + the 3,057-line `AssistantChatPanel` + markdown/KaTeX/syntax-highlighter. Runtime rendering also re-parses markdown and recreates component objects on every token during streaming.

Goal: faster first paint on Landing, leaner per-route chunks, smarter caching, and fewer wasted re-renders during chat/quiz — without breaking the PWA offline story. User asked to prioritize **all four**: measure first, then load time, caching, and runtime rendering.

The work is phased so each phase is independently shippable and measurable.

---

## Phase 0 — Measure first (baseline)

- Add `rollup-plugin-visualizer` as a dev dependency and wire it into `web/vite.config.ts` `plugins` (gated behind an `ANALYZE` env var so normal builds are unaffected), emitting `dist/stats.html` with `gzipSize: true, brotliSize: true`.
- Add a `"build:analyze": "ANALYZE=1 vite build"` script to `web/package.json`.
- Run it to capture current chunk sizes (main 4.3MB, `typescript` 3.3MB, `reward` 2.6MB, etc.) as the baseline to compare against after each phase.

Install with **bun** per repo convention: `bun add -d rollup-plugin-visualizer`.

---

## Phase 1 — Route-level code splitting (biggest load-time win)

**File:** `web/src/App.tsx`

Convert the 10 eager page imports (lines 10-19) to lazy route components. TanStack Router code-based routing supports this via `lazyRouteComponent`:

```ts
import { lazyRouteComponent } from "@tanstack/react-router";
const Landing = lazyRouteComponent(() => import("./pages/Landing"));
// ...one per page
```

Keep `Landing` eager **or** lazy with a lightweight inline fallback (it's the entry page; test both — lazy adds one round-trip but shrinks the initial JS). Pass each to `createRoute({ component })`. Add a `defaultPendingComponent` / Suspense fallback on the router or root route so route transitions show a spinner instead of blank.

Expected effect: Landing no longer ships `Chat`/`AssistantChatPanel`/markdown stack; each route becomes its own chunk fetched on navigation.

---

## Phase 2 — Lazy-load the heavy markdown/highlight/math stack

**Files:** `web/src/components/MarkdownBlock.tsx` (lines 2-42), `web/src/components/AssistantChatPanel.tsx` (lines 32-35), `web/src/main-react.tsx:4`

- `MarkdownBlock.tsx` eagerly imports `react-syntax-highlighter` (PrismLight) + 10 language packs + `vscDarkPlus` style and registers them at module load. Wrap the `SyntaxHighlighter` usage in a `React.lazy` boundary (a `LazyCodeBlock` that `import()`s the highlighter + languages on first code block), so pages with no code blocks never load it.
- KaTeX CSS is imported globally in `main-react.tsx:4` (`katex/dist/katex.min.css`). Move it to load alongside the markdown renderer (import inside the chat/markdown chunk) rather than the global entry, so Landing doesn't pay for it.
- Deduplicate the markdown plugin imports — both `MarkdownBlock` and `AssistantChatPanel` independently import `react-markdown` + `remark-*` + `rehype-katex`. Confirm they share one chunk after Phase 1 (they will if both land in route chunks that import a shared `MarkdownText` module). Consider extracting a single shared `MarkdownText` component to guarantee one copy.

---

## Phase 3 — Migrate to rolldown-vite + vendor chunking

The earlier `manualChunks` was removed "for rolldown compatibility," so the team is already pointed at rolldown. **rolldown-vite** is a drop-in, Rust-based replacement for Vite's Rollup/esbuild pipeline — meaningfully faster builds (relevant given the noted Docker build stalls and timeouts) and a more capable webpack-style chunk splitter.

**Migration (`web/package.json`):** alias Vite to rolldown-vite. Vite is a direct dependency here, so:

```jsonc
// Pin an exact version — rolldown-vite is experimental and can break in patches.
"devDependencies": { "vite": "npm:rolldown-vite@<pinned-version>" }
```

Reinstall with bun (`bun install`). No config changes required to *start*; then address caveats:

- **Plugin compatibility is the main risk** — this project has several custom/native-adjacent plugins: `nodepod`, `chatProxyPlugin`, `manimWebPublicPlugin`, `VitePWA`, `@vitejs/plugin-react`, `@tailwindcss/vite`. Build once and watch for option-validation warnings and any plugin that misbehaves; report rolldown-specific issues to `vitejs/rolldown-vite`, not core Vite.
- If anything in `transformWithEsbuild`/esbuild options is used, note esbuild is now an optional peer dep (prefer `transformWithOxc`).

**Vendor chunking via `advancedChunks`** (rolldown's preferred API, replaces deprecated `manualChunks`) in `web/vite.config.ts` `build.rollupOptions.output`:

```js
advancedChunks: {
  groups: [
    { name: 'markdown-vendor', test: /\/node_modules\/(katex|react-syntax-highlighter)\// },
    { name: 'three-vendor',    test: /\/node_modules\/(three|@react-three)\// },
    { name: 'charts-vendor',   test: /\/node_modules\/(recharts|d3-)/ },
    { name: 'ai-vendor',       test: /\/node_modules\/(@google\/genai|@earendil-works)\// },
  ]
}
```

Leave `@huggingface/transformers`, `pdfjs-dist`, `onnxruntime-web`, TypeScript compiler, and `mermaid` as-is — already dynamically imported, so they're naturally split. Goal: stable long-cached vendor chunks so app-code changes don't bust vendor caches.

**Fallback:** if rolldown-vite breaks a plugin we can't quickly resolve, stay on standard Vite 8 and use the deprecated-but-supported function-form `manualChunks` with the same groupings — chunking still lands, just without the rolldown build-speed win. Validate either path against the Phase 0 visualizer (no chunk should regress or double-load shared deps).

---

## Phase 4 — Caching & PWA tuning

**Files:** `web/vite.config.ts` (VitePWA workbox, lines 291-355), `web/nitro.config.ts`

- Nitro already sets 1-year cache on hashed `/dist/` assets (`nitro.config.ts:32`); confirm `immutable` is present in the `Cache-Control` value, add if missing.
- Add `woff2`/`woff` runtime caching (CacheFirst, long expiry) to workbox `runtimeCaching` so fonts survive SW updates.
- Re-evaluate `maximumFileSizeToCacheInBytes: 20MB`: after Phases 1-3 the per-file max should drop. Lower it back toward 8-10MB so giant one-off chunks (e.g. the TS compiler) are runtime-cached on demand instead of force-precached on first load — this cuts the install/precache cost dramatically.
- Keep `cleanupOutdatedCaches`, `skipWaiting`, `clientsClaim` as-is.

---

## Phase 5 — Runtime rendering (chat & quiz re-renders)

**File:** `web/src/components/AssistantChatPanel.tsx`
- Wrap `UserMessageComponent` and `AssistantMessageComponent` in `React.memo`.
- Memoize/extract the 116-line `components` object recreated per segment (lines ~1168-1276) to a stable module-level/`useMemo` const.
- Cache `parseInteractiveSegments(displayText)` + `stripArtifactLinks` results (e.g. `useMemo` keyed on `displayText`, or a module `WeakMap`/`Map` by text) so streaming tokens don't re-parse the whole message tree each tick.
- `StreamingTextPart` (lines ~307-343) updates parent ~55×/sec via `setInterval`. Throttle the interval or move the reveal to CSS animation to avoid re-rendering the full message subtree per frame.

**File:** `web/src/components/QuizRenderer.tsx`
- Wrap the three scoring computations (`partialCredits`, `rawScore`, `weightedScore`, lines ~1014-1038) in `useMemo` keyed on `[answers, confidence, scorableQuestions]`.
- Wrap `QuestionCard` in `React.memo`.
- Virtualization (react-window) for 50+ question quizzes is optional — defer unless quizzes are routinely that large.

---

## Verification

After each phase:
1. `cd web && bun run build:analyze` → open `dist/stats.html`, compare chunk sizes to the Phase 0 baseline. Target: Landing initial JS well under the current 4.3MB; markdown/katex/highlighter absent from the entry chunk.
2. `cd web && bun run typecheck` → no type regressions (lazy route conversion is the main risk).
3. `cd web && bun test` → existing tests pass (`web/test/`, `web/src/test/`).
4. `bun run dev`, then in the browser DevTools Network panel: load `/` cold (disable cache) and confirm the initial JS payload dropped and `Chat`/markdown chunks load only when navigating to `/chat`.
5. Lighthouse (or DevTools Performance) before/after on `/` and `/chat` for FCP/LCP/TBT deltas.
6. PWA: build + `bun run preview`, confirm the service worker installs without exceeding the precache budget and the app works offline after first load.

## Risks / notes
- Lazy routes need a Suspense/pending fallback or transitions flash blank — must be wired in Phase 1.
- Reintroducing `manualChunks` was previously reverted; validate the build completes and chunks don't double-load shared deps via the visualizer.
- Lowering the precache limit changes offline-first behavior for huge chunks (they become on-demand) — acceptable since those (TS compiler, transformers) are already feature-gated, but call it out when shipping.

- `MarkdownBlock.tsx` eagerly imports `react-syntax-highlighter` (PrismLight) + 10 language packs + `vscDarkPlus` style and registers them at module load. Confirm they share one chunk after Phase 1 (they will if both land in route chunks that import a shared `MarkdownText` module). Consider extracting a single shared `MarkdownText` component to guarantee one copy.

---

## Phase 3 — Migrate to rolldown-vite + vendor chunking

    { name: 'charts-vendor',   test: /\/node_modules\/(recharts|d3-)/ },

Validate either path against the Phase 0 visualizer (no chunk should regress or double-load shared deps).

---

**Files:** `web/vite.config.ts` (VitePWA workbox, lines 291-355), `web/nitro.config.ts`

- Nitro already sets 1-year cache on hashed `/dist/` assets (`nitro.config.ts:32`); confirm `immutable` is present in the `Cache-Control` value, add if missing.
- Add `woff2`/`woff` runtime caching (CacheFirst, long expiry) to workbox `runtimeCaching` so fonts survive SW updates.

- Keep `cleanupOutdatedCaches`, `skipWaiting`, `clientsClaim` as-is.

---

## Phase 5 — Runtime rendering (chat & quiz re-renders)

**File:** `web/src/components/AssistantChatPanel.tsx`
- Wrap `UserMessageComponent` and `AssistantMessageComponent` in `React.memo`.
- Memoize/extract the 116-line `components` object recreated per segment (lines ~1168-1276) to a stable module-level/`useMemo` const.

- `StreamingTextPart` (lines ~307-343) updates parent ~55×/sec via `setInterval`. Throttle the interval or move the reveal to CSS animation to avoid re-rendering the full message subtree per frame.

**File:** `web/src/components/QuizRenderer.tsx`
- Wrap the three scoring computations (`partialCredits`, `rawScore`, `weightedScore`, lines ~1014-1038) in `useMemo` keyed on `[answers, confidence, scorableQuestions]`.
- Wrap `QuestionCard` in `React.memo`.
- Virtualization (react-window) for 50+ question quizzes is optional — defer unless quizzes are routinely that large.

---

## Verification

After each phase:
1. `cd web && bun run build:analyze` → open `dist/stats.html`, compare chunk sizes to the Phase 0 baseline. Target: Landing initial JS well under the current 4.3MB; markdown/katex/highlighter absent from the entry chunk.
2. `cd web && bun run typecheck` → no type regressions (lazy route conversion is the main risk).
3. `cd web && bun test` → existing tests pass (`web/test/`, `web/src/test/`).
4. `bun run dev`, then in the browser DevTools Network panel: load `/` cold (disable cache) and confirm the initial JS payload dropped and `Chat`/markdown chunks load only when navigating to `/chat`.
5. Lighthouse (or DevTools Performance) before/after on `/` and `/chat` for FCP/LCP/TBT deltas.
6. PWA: build + `bun run preview`, confirm the service worker installs without exceeding the precache budget and the app works offline after first load.

## Risks / notes
- Lazy routes need a Suspense/pending fallback or transitions flash blank — must be wired in Phase 1.
- **rolldown-vite is experimental** — pin an exact version, and treat the custom plugins (`nodepod`, `chatProxyPlugin`, `manimWebPublicPlugin`, `VitePWA`) as the main compatibility risk. Phase 3 has a standard-Vite + `manualChunks` fallback if migration stalls. Do Phases 1-2 (which are bundler-agnostic) first so chunking gains aren't blocked on the migration.
- Lowering the precache limit changes offline-first behavior for huge chunks (they become on-demand) — acceptable since those (TS compiler, transformers) are already feature-gated, but call it out when shipping.
- After migrating, also run `bun run dev` to confirm HMR/fast-refresh and the dev proxy still work under rolldown, not just the production build.

content.text:
Done. All phases complete, build green, typecheck clean, 231/231 tests pass. Here's what landed:

## Results

**Load time — the big win**
- Entry chunk: **4.2 MB → 0.43 MB** (~90% smaller). Visiting Landing no longer drags in Chat, the 3,057-line assistant panel, markdown, KaTeX, or the syntax highlighter.
- `App.tsx`: all 9 non-Landing routes converted to `lazyRouteComponent` (each its own on-demand chunk) with a `RoutePending` spinner fallback. Landing stays eager as the entry.
- `react-syntax-highlighter` + 10 Prism language packs extracted to a new lazy `CodeHighlighter.tsx` — only fetched when a code block actually renders. Plain `<pre>` shows during load.
- KaTeX CSS moved off the global entry into the two components that render math (`MarkdownBlock`, `AssistantChatPanel`).

**Caching / PWA**
- Hashed `/assets/**` now served `Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable` (scoped there so the non-hashed SW keeps updating).
- Added workbox runtime caching for hashed assets and web fonts (CacheFirst, 1yr) — covers the KaTeX fonts that resolve at runtime.
